title: dracontia kelloggii
intro: Dracontia kelloggii, also known as the Kellogg's dragon root or Pacific dragon root, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It is native to the Pacific coast of North America, and has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight off infections, making it beneficial for various health issues, including wounds, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
